Quick Reference

Membership Credit Callback API

This is the short implementation reference for the membership backend team. It covers only the callback that the top-up system sends after a shop owner approves a top-up.

Top-up system → Membership system

Behavior

  • the callback is sent only when a shop owner approves a transaction
  • the top-up system calls the membership API before finalizing the approval
  • if the membership API returns HTTP `2xx`, the top-up is marked approved
  • if the membership API returns non-`2xx` or times out, the approval is blocked

Request spec

Item Value
MethodPOST
URLYour configured membership credit endpoint
Content-Typeapplication/json
Event headerX-Topup-Event: topup_approved
Timestamp headerX-Topup-Timestamp
Signature headerX-Topup-Signature

Signature rule

The top-up system signs the callback request body with HMAC SHA256 using the shared secret MEMBERSHIP_SYNC_SECRET.

Signed string

{timestamp}.{rawBody}

Digest format

hex

`timestamp` is the exact value from `X-Topup-Timestamp`. `rawBody` means the original JSON string before parsing.

Headers

Header Description
Content-Typeapplication/json
X-Topup-EventFixed value: topup_approved
X-Topup-TimestampISO timestamp used in signature generation
X-Topup-SignatureHMAC SHA256 hex digest of {timestamp}.{rawBody}

Payload example

{
  "event": "topup_approved",
  "transaction_id": "9c5f6947-4d8d-4c47-a725-2c4d44a7f0f3",
  "member_code": "63936541",
  "total_amount": 300,
  "currency": "MOP",
  "item_count": 2,
  "approved_at": "2026-07-01T10:15:30.000Z",
  "owner_login": "60000000",
  "shop": {
    "id": "local-shop-uuid",
    "code": "POC001",
    "name": "表嫂美食",
    "external_shop_id": "shop-001"
  },
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "line-item-id",
      "merchant_name": "表嫂美食",
      "transaction_order_no": "2026062411365300000010",
      "amount": 300,
      "transaction_time": "2026-06-24 11:40:00",
      "payment_method": "中國銀行澳門分行(6756)"
    }
  ]
}

Expected membership behavior

  1. verify the `X-Topup-Signature` header using the shared secret
  2. validate the event is `topup_approved`
  3. locate the customer by `member_code`
  4. credit `total_amount` into the customer account
  5. store your own membership-side reference ID for audit
  6. return HTTP `2xx` only after the credit is successfully completed

Success response

The top-up system treats any HTTP `2xx` response as success. Recommended response body:

{
  "ok": true,
  "credited": true,
  "reference_id": "membership-credit-000123"
}

If you return `2xx`, the top-up record becomes approved.

Failure behavior

  • non-`2xx` response blocks approval
  • timeout blocks approval
  • signature verification failure should return `401` or `403`
  • business validation failure should return `400` or `409` with a readable message

If the membership API fails, the top-up system does not approve the transaction.

Verification pseudocode

import crypto from "node:crypto";

function verifyTopupCallbackSignature(secret, timestamp, rawBody, receivedSignature) {
  const expected = crypto
    .createHmac("sha256", secret)
    .update(`${timestamp}.${rawBody}`)
    .digest("hex");

  return expected === receivedSignature;
}
